subject: Biology
title: Microbes and Mental Health
description: A micro-lesson that makes Microbes and Mental Health usable.

hook.text: Your gut and your brain are connected by the vagus nerve—a direct communication highway.

buildup.text: Gut bacteria produce neurotransmitters like serotonin and GABA that influence mood.

discovery.text: About 90% of the body's serotonin is made in the gut, not the brain.

twist.text: Mice given specific probiotics showed reduced anxiety. The effect vanished when the vagus nerve was cut.

climax.text: The gut-brain axis is now a major research frontier in psychiatry and neuroscience.

punchline.text: Your gut feelings might be literally real.

quiz.question: Where is most of the body's serotonin produced?
